tree.setup: Friday night at a KTV (karaoke). Your friend group has split into factions: Xiao Dan wants to sing 90s Chinese pop, Xiao Hao insists on English songs, and the room has been booked for three hours. Xiao Dan holds the remote control like a scepter. The screen glows with a queue of songs. The fruit platter is untouched because nobody can agree on the first song.
tree.setup_zh: 周五晚上KTV。你们的朋友圈分裂了：小丹要唱九十年代华语流行，小浩坚持唱英文歌，包间订了三个小时。小丹像拿权杖一样拿着遥控器。屏幕上闪着待播歌曲。水果拼盘没人动，因为第一首歌都定不下来。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 先唱周杰伦！不会唱周杰伦来什么KTV？
turns.text_pinyin: xiān chàng zhōujiélún! bú huì chàng zhōujiélún lái shénme KTV?
turns.text_en: Jay Chou first! What's the point of KTV if you can't sing Jay Chou?

options.text_zh: 这样吧，一人选两首，轮流唱。先来一首大家都会的热场！
options.text_pinyin: zhèyàng ba, yì rén xuǎn liǎng shǒu, lúnliú chàng. xiān lái yì shǒu dàjiā dōu huì de rèchǎng!
options.text_en: How about this -- each person picks two songs, we take turns. Start with one everyone knows to warm up!
options.feedback: Both factions pause. Xiao Dan nods.「行。那先唱《朋友》，大家都会。」轮流 is 'take turns.' 热场 is 'warm up the atmosphere.' Diplomacy through structure.

options.text_zh: 周杰伦可以啊！小浩，你先让一下，下一首你选。
options.text_pinyin: zhōujiélún kěyǐ a! xiǎo hào, nǐ xiān ràng yíxià, xià yì shǒu nǐ xuǎn.
options.text_en: Jay Chou works! Xiao Hao, give in for now, you pick next.
options.feedback: Xiao Hao grumbles but accepts.「行吧。但下一首是我的。」让一下 is 'give way/compromise.' Picking sides works short-term but you need a system.

options.text_zh: 你们别吵了。先吃水果，想好了再唱。
options.text_pinyin: nǐmen bié chǎo le. xiān chī shuǐguǒ, xiǎng hǎo le zài chàng.
options.text_en: Stop arguing. Eat fruit first, sing when you've decided.
options.feedback: Nobody touches the fruit. 别吵了 is 'stop arguing.' Delaying the decision doesn't solve it -- the clock is ticking on three hours of booth rental.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好好好，轮流！我先来一首《晴天》。你要不要一起唱？我可以唱副歌，你唱主歌。
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo hǎo hǎo, lúnliú! wǒ xiān lái yì shǒu 'qíngtiān.' nǐ yào bu yào yìqǐ chàng? wǒ kěyǐ chàng fùgē, nǐ chàng zhǔgē.
turns.text_en: OK OK, taking turns! I'll start with 'Sunny Day.' Want to sing together? I can do the chorus, you do the verses.

options.text_zh: 好啊！不过我不太会唱中文歌。歌词在屏幕上有拼音吗？
options.text_pinyin: hǎo a! búguò wǒ bú tài huì chàng zhōngwén gē. gēcí zài píngmù shàng yǒu pīnyīn ma?
options.text_en: Sure! But I'm not great at Chinese songs. Do the lyrics on screen have pinyin?
options.feedback: 「有的有，有的没有。没有的话你跟着我唱就行了。」歌词 is 'lyrics.' KTV screens show characters that light up with the music -- with or without pinyin.

options.text_zh: 我先听你唱一遍，下一轮我再试试。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ xiān tīng nǐ chàng yí biàn, xià yì lún wǒ zài shìshi.
options.text_en: Let me hear you sing it once first, I'll try next round.
options.feedback: 「好，你先听！」Xiao Dan grabs the mic with confidence. 听一遍 is 'listen once.' Learning the melody first is a safe strategy.

options.text_zh: 我唱歌很难听的……你们唱，我鼓掌。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ chàng gē hěn nántīng de... nǐmen chàng, wǒ gǔzhǎng.
options.text_en: My singing is terrible... You guys sing, I'll clap.
options.feedback: 「KTV不是比赛！唱得难听才好玩！」Xiao Dan shoves the mic toward you. 难听 is 'bad sounding.' 鼓掌 is 'clap.' At KTV, enthusiasm matters more than talent.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不错不错！你的发音其实挺好的。来，到你选歌了。你要点什么？中文英文都行。
turns.text_pinyin: búcuò búcuò! nǐ de fāyīn qíshí tǐng hǎo de. lái, dào nǐ xuǎn gē le. nǐ yào diǎn shénme? zhōngwén yīngwén dōu xíng.
turns.text_en: Not bad! Your pronunciation is actually pretty good. Come on, your turn to pick. What do you want? Chinese or English, either is fine.

options.text_zh: 我点一首《月亮代表我的心》吧。这首歌我练过，比较有信心。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ diǎn yì shǒu 'yuèliang dàibiǎo wǒ de xīn' ba. zhè shǒu gē wǒ liàn guo, bǐjiào yǒu xìnxīn.
options.text_en: I'll pick 'The Moon Represents My Heart.' I've practiced this one, I'm more confident.
options.feedback: The room erupts.「经典！」Everyone knows this song. Xiao Dan and Xiao Hao both sing along. 点歌 is 'to select a song.' 练过 means 'have practiced.' Choosing a classic unites any KTV room.

options.text_zh: 有没有节奏比较简单的中文歌？我怕跟不上拍子。
options.text_pinyin: yǒu méiyǒu jiézòu bǐjiào jiǎndān de zhōngwén gē? wǒ pà gēn bu shàng pāizi.
options.text_en: Are there any Chinese songs with simpler rhythm? I'm afraid I can't keep the beat.
options.feedback: 「《小幸运》节奏慢，歌词也简单！」Xiao Dan scrolls through suggestions. 节奏 is 'rhythm.' 跟不上拍子 is 'can't keep up with the beat.' Asking for a recommendation shows you want to participate.

options.text_zh: 我唱一首英文歌吧。小浩你一起来！
options.text_pinyin: wǒ chàng yì shǒu yīngwén gē ba. xiǎo hào nǐ yìqǐ lái!
options.text_en: I'll sing an English song. Xiao Hao, join me!
options.feedback: Xiao Hao jumps up.「终于！」Xiao Dan rolls her eyes but starts eating watermelon. 一起来 is 'join in together.' Bringing in the person who felt left out is socially smooth.

tree.cultural_note: KTV (卡拉OK) is a staple of Chinese social life. Private rooms (包间, bāojiān) are rented by the hour. 点歌 (diǎn gē) means selecting songs from the system. Classic crowd-pleasers include《月亮代表我的心》and anything by 周杰伦 (Jay Chou). Nobody actually cares if you sing badly -- it's about participation and fun.
